title: Power BI Visualizations, Charts, and DAX Measure Patterns

confirmed_facts.fact: Power BI visual types by category: Comparison (Clustered Bar/Column, Stacked, Combo, Ribbon), Trend (Line, Area), Part-to-whole (Pie, Donut, Treemap, 100% Stacked), Correlation (Scatter/Bubble), KPI (Card, Multi-row Card, KPI Visual, Gauge), Detailed Data (Table, Matrix), Filtering (Slicer), Geographic (Map, Filled Map), AI (Decomposition Tree, Key Influencers, Smart Narrative)
CALCULATE is the most important DAX function — evaluates an expression in a modified filter context. Syntax: CALCULATE(expression, [filter1], [filter2], ...). Filters are AND-combined.

Measures vs Calculated Columns: Measures are computed at query time and adapt to filter context (slicers, visual axes). Calculated columns are computed row-by-row at data refresh time and stored in the model. Use measures for aggregations, columns for row-level lookups.
VAR/RETURN syntax makes DAX more readable and performant by evaluating an expression once and reusing it. VAR is evaluated in the filter context where it is defined, not where it is used in RETURN.

ALL() removes all filters from a table or column — used for % of total calculations. ALLEXCEPT() removes all filters except specified columns. ALLSELECTED() removes visual-level filters while keeping slicer selections.

RELATED() follows a many-to-one relationship to bring a value from a related table into the current row context (calculated columns). RELATEDTABLE() returns all rows in a related table matching the current row context (measures).

Star schema is the recommended data model: fact tables (large, many rows) connected to dimension tables (small, lookup) via many-to-one relationships. All relationships radiate outward from fact tables to dimensions. Single cross-filter direction preferred.

Cross-filtering: by default, selecting a data point in one visual cross-filters all other visuals on the page. Control via Format > Edit Interactions: choose Filter, Highlight, or None for each visual pair.

implementation_notes:
Visual selection guide for security dashboards:
- KPI cards (top row): Card or New Card visual — one per key metric, show current value + vs-prior comparison
- Trend over time: Line chart — X axis = DateTable[MonthYear], Y axis = [Metric Value], Legend = MetricDim[MetricName]
- Severity distribution: Stacked Column or Donut — group by severity level, value = count
- Multi-metric comparison matrix: Matrix visual — Rows = MetricDim[MetricName], Columns = DateTable[MonthYear], Values = [Metric Value]
- Detail drill-down: Table visual with conditional formatting, linked via drill-through
- Period selection: Slicer (Dropdown style for compact header)

Core DAX aggregation functions:
```dax
// Basic aggregations
Total Sales = SUM(Sales[Amount])
Unique Hosts = DISTINCTCOUNT(Detections[host_id])
Row Count = COUNTROWS(FactTable)

// Iterator aggregations (row-by-row evaluation then aggregate)
Weighted Score = SUMX(Detections, Detections[severity] * Detections[qds])
Avg Per Host = AVERAGEX(VALUES(Detections[host_id]), [Total Vulns])
```

FORMAT function for display:
```dax
FORMAT([Total Sales], "$#,##0")          -- $1,234
FORMAT([MoM Change %], "0.0%")           -- 12.3%
FORMAT(MAX(DateTable[Date]), "MMMM YYYY") -- January 2026
FORMAT([Count], "#,##0")                 -- 1,234
```

Conditional formatting setup for tables/matrices:
- Background color: Format pane > Cell elements > Background color > Rules or Gradient
- Icon sets: Format pane > Cell elements > Icons > Rules (Layout = 'Data only' for icon-only columns)
- Data bars: Format pane > Cell elements > Data bars (in-cell proportional bars)
- RAG thresholds: If value >= Red → #FF4444, If value >= Amber → #FFC000, else → #70AD47

Report design best practices:
- Consistent grid layout: Title top, KPI cards below, main chart middle, detail table/filters bottom
- Limit to 3-5 colors; use one highlight color for current period, one for comparison
- Dynamic titles: Title Measure = "Dashboard — " & SELECTEDVALUE(DateTable[MonthYear], "All Periods")
- Drill-through: right-click data point → navigate to detail page filtered to that context
- Bookmarks: capture visual/filter state for reset buttons or view switching
- Tooltip pages: create a small report page (set size to Tooltip), assign to visuals via Format > Tooltips > Page
caveats: Avoid calculated columns on large tables — use measures instead for better performance
DISTINCTCOUNT on large high-cardinality columns is expensive — consider pre-aggregating
Avoid bidirectional many-to-many relationships — causes unexpected cross-filtering and ambiguity errors
Only one relationship can be active between two tables — use USERELATIONSHIP() for alternatives
Pie charts work poorly with >6 categories or similar-sized slices — use Treemap or Bar chart instead
Cross-filtering defaults may confuse users — explicitly set Edit Interactions for each visual pair
R/Python visuals require a local R/Python installation and do not work in Power BI Service without a gateway
